book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
Extracts from the Acts. Session II.
title: Extracts from the Acts. Session II.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: The most pious and God-beloved bishops, Arcadius and Projectus, as also the most beloved-of-God Philip, a presbyter and legate of the Apostolic See, then entered and took their seats.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Philip the presbyter and legate of the Apostolic See said: We bless the holy and adorable Trinity that our lowliness has been deemed worthy to attend your holy Synod.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: For a long time ago (πάλαι) our most holy and blessed pope Cœlestine, bishop of the Apostolic See, through his letters to that holy and most pious man Cyril, bishop of Alexandria, gave judgment concerning the present cause and affair (ὥρισεν) which letters have been shown to your holy assembly.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: And now again for the corroboration of the Catholic (καθολικῆς) faith, he has sent through us letters to all your holinesses, which you will bid (κελούσατε) to be read with becoming reverence (πρεπόντως) and to be entered on the ecclesiastical minutes.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Arcadius, a bishop and legate of the Roman Church said: May it please your blessedness to give order that the letters of the holy and ever-to-be-mentioned-with-veneration Pope Cœlestine, bishop of the Apostolic See, which have been brought by us, be read, from which your reverence will be able to see what care he has for all the Churches.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Projectus, a bishop and legate of the Roman Church said, May it please, etc. [The same as Arcadius had said verbatim!]

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: And afterwards the most holy and beloved-of-God Cyril, bishop of the Church of Alexandria, spoke as is next in order contained; Siricius, notary of the holy Catholic (καθολικῆς) Church of Rome read it.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Cyril, the bishop of Alexandria said: Let the letter received from the most holy and altogether most blessed Cœlestine, bishop of the Apostolic See of Rome be read to the holy Synod with fitting honour.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Siricius, notary of the holy Catholic (καθολικῆς) Church of the city of Rome read it.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: And after it was read in Latin, Juvenal, the bishop of Jerusalem said: Let the writings of the most holy and blessed bishop of great Rome which have just been read, be entered on the minutes.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: And all the most reverend bishops prayed that the letter might be translated and read.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Philip, the presbyter of the Apostolic See and Legate said: The custom has been sufficiently complied with, that the writings of the Apostolic See should first be read in Latin.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: But now since your holiness has demanded that they be read in Greek also, it is necessary that your holiness’s desire should be satisfied; We have taken care that this be done, and that the Latin be turned into Greek.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Give order therefore that it be received and read in your holy hearing.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Arcadius and Projectus, bishops and legates said, As your blessedness ordered that the writings which we brought should be brought to the knowledge of all, for of our holy brethren bishops there are not a few who do not understand Latin, therefore the letter has been translated into Greek and if you so command let it be read.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Flavian, the bishop of Philippi said: Let the translation of the letter of the most holy and beloved of God, bishop of the Roman Church be received and read.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Peter, the presbyter of Alexandria and primicerius of the notaries read as follows:
